task_updates.executor_notes: Updated `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx` with left/right 8px pointer resize handles on each action wrapper. The component now owns transient resize geometry in `resizeOverrides`, mirrored in `resizePreviewRef` so pointerup uses the latest drag position without waiting for React state. Pointerdown captures the handle pointer, fires `onActionResizeStart({ action, row, dir })`, pointermove updates the transient geometry and fires `onActionResizing({ action, row, start, end, dir })`, and pointerup fires `onActionResizeEnd({ action, row, start, end, dir })` before clearing the override. This preserves `useClipResize.ts` callback compatibility without any hook changes. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exits 0.

Updated `src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx` to resolve the new `.timeline-canvas-edit-area` first and treat that scroll container as the authoritative grid for `scrollLeft`/`scrollTop`. The lookup logic still falls back to `.timeline-editor-edit-area` and `.ReactVirtualized__Grid` so the current library-backed editor keeps working until T6 swaps components. `getTimelineDomNodes()` cache validation now checks `isConnected` and `wrapper.contains(node)` before reusing cached nodes, so DOM swaps invalidate stale references correctly. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exits 0.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: The canvas now keeps active resize geometry in a transient override map that is consulted during rendering, so clips resize live instead of snapping on release. The override is cleared on pointerup/pointercancel, and the final callback uses the latest geometry from a ref-backed preview cache. Resize callbacks still use the same `{ action, row, dir }` core shape expected by `useClipResize.ts`, with extra `start/end` fields matching the former library contract.

Drop-position lookups now prefer `.timeline-canvas-edit-area` and use that same element for scroll offsets, while still falling back to the legacy `.timeline-editor-edit-area`/`.ReactVirtualized__Grid` path until T6. `TimelineEditor.clientXToTime()` was updated with the same selector order, and `getTimelineDomNodes()` now invalidates stale cached nodes when the wrapper DOM changes. The shared `computeDropPosition()` path used by drag coordinator and external drop still compiles cleanly with the migrated selectors.
